title: Types of unemployment and the natural rate of unemployment
description: Practice what you have learned about frictional, structural, and cyclical unemployment and the natural rate of unemployment in this exercise.
topic_path: Economic indicators and the business cycle
Types of unemployment and the natural rate of unemployment

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes an increase in structural unemployment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: People lose jobs in agriculture when the growing season ends
People become less willing to move to another state for a job
An economy enters a recession
People must undergo a long retraining process in new skills after losing their jobs
More job-search websites are created to better match job seekers with jobs

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume the government of Hamsterville increases taxation and reduces government spending. These actions cause a fall in total spending in the economy.

**Which of the following is the best description of the type of unemployment the fall in output will have?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Structural unemployment
Frictional unemployment
Seasonal unemployment
Cyclical unemployment
The natural rate of unemployment (NRU)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following correctly describes an individual who is frictionally unemployed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A tax accountant is laid off as more people use software to do their taxes
A new college graduate is looking for their first job
A worker in a truck factory is laid off due to less spending across the whole economy
A full time retail clerk has her hours reduced to part time by her manager
An individual who has been looking for work gives up the job search

assessment_items.question_content:
Hercules lost his job as a tailor after his company purchased robotic sewing machines to replace him. Now he cannot find a job because nobody wants to hire tailors.

**Which of the following best describes Hercules’ employment situation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Structurally unemployed
Seasonally unemployed
Frictionally unemployed
Cyclically unemployed
Not in the labor force

assessment_items.question_content:
Angelica is unsatisfied with how well her job as an urban planner pays and quits to find another job. 

**Which of the following best describes Angelica’s employment situation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Frictionally unemployed
Seasonally unemployed
Structurally unemployed
Cyclically unemployed
Not in the labor force

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the concept of the natural rate of unemployment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: When the unemployment rate is equal to zero
The amount of unemployment that exists when producing inside its production possibility curve (PPC)
The amount of unemployment that exists when producing outside its production possibility curve (PPC)
The unemployment that exists due to changes in nature, such as changing from summer to winter
The unemployment rate that is associated with producing the full-employment level of output

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes what is included in the natural rate of unemployment (NRU)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: frictional unemployment and cyclical unemployment
frictional unemployment and structural unemployment
structural unemployment and seasonal unemployment
structural unemployment and cyclical unemployment
seasonal unemployment and cyclical unemployment

assessment_items.question_content:
The current rate of unemployment in Burrland is 3%. Economists estimate that frictional unemployment is 5% and structural unemployment is 2%. 

**Which of the following best describes the amount of cyclical unemployment in Burrland and current output in the economy?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Cyclical unemployment is the difference between the current rate of unemployment and the natural rate of unemployment:

$\text{Cyclical unemployment} = \text{Current rate of unemployment} - \text{Natural rate of unemployment}$
The natural rate of unemployment is the sum of frictional unemployment and structural unemployment:

$\text{Natural rate of unemployment} = \text{Frictional unemployment} + \text{Structural unemployment}$
The natural rate of unemployment in this economy is 7%:

$\begin{align}\text{Natural rate of unemployment} &= 5\% + 2\%\\\\
&=7\%\end{align}$
The cyclical unemployment in this economy is $-4\%$:

$\begin{align}\text{Cyclical unemployment} &= 3\% - 7\%\\\\
&=-4\%\end{align}$

An economy is producing more than full employment output if its cyclical unemployment is negative, or in other words, if the natural rate of unemployment is greater than the current rate of unemployment. Since 7% is greater than 3%, Burrland’s economy is producing more than full employment output.

choices.content: Cyclical unemployment is −4%; output is greater than full employment output.
Cyclical unemployment is −4%; output is less than full employment output.
Cyclical unemployment is 0%; output is equal to full employment output.
Cyclical unemployment is 0%, output is less than full employment output.
Cyclical unemployment is 7%; output is equal to full employment output.

assessment_items.question_content:
The Production Possibilities Curve (PPC) for the Federation of St. Kitts and Nevis is shown here, where X represents current production: 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/698a2ce109674d381bea1cb34cc29f307d13093f)


The table below contains economic data from the Federation of St. Kitts and Nevis:

Measure of unemployment | Value 
-|-
Natural rate of unemployment | 9%
Frictional unemployment | 3%

**Which of the following best describes structural unemployment and the current rate of unemployment based on the PPC and the data in the table?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Structural unemployment is 6%; current rate of unemployment is greater than 9%.
Structural unemployment is 6%; current rate of unemployment is less than 9%.
Structural unemployment is 12%; current rate of unemployment is greater than 9%.
Structural unemployment is 12%; current rate of unemployment is equal to 9%.
Structural unemployment is 6%; current rate of unemployment is equal to 9%.

assessment_items.question_content:
The seasonally adjusted rate of unemployment in Hamsterville is $7\%$. But the natural rate of unemployment there is $5\%$.

**Which of the following describes why there is a difference between the current rate of unemployment and the natural rate of unemployment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is $2\%$ Cyclical unemployment
There is $2\%$ Structural unemployment
There is $2\%$ frictional unemployment
There is 2% seasonal unemployment
We need more information to determine why there is a difference

assessment_items.question_content:
**If an economy is producing less than its full employment output, which of the following statements about unemployment best describes this economy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There will be structural and frictional unemployment, but no cyclical unemployment
There will be structural, frictional, and cyclical unemployment
There will be structural and cyclical unemployment, but no frictional unemployment
There will be cyclical unemployment, but no frictional or structural unemployment
There will be cyclical and frictional unemployment, but no structural unemployment

assessment_items.question_content:
The current rate of unemployment in Hamiltonia is 7\%. Economists estimate that frictional unemployment is 4\% and structural unemployment is 1\%. 

**Which of the following best describes the amount of cyclical unemployment in Hamiltonia and current output in the economy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Cyclical unemployment is the difference between the current rate of unemployment and the natural rate of unemployment:

$\text{Cyclical unemployment} = \text{Current rate of unemployment} - \text{Natural rate of unemployment}$
The natural rate of unemployment is the sum of frictional unemployment and structural unemployment:

$\text{Natural rate of unemployment} = \text{Frictional unemployment} + \text{Structural unemployment}$
The natural rate of unemployment in this economy is 5%:

$\begin{align}\text{Natural rate of unemployment} &= 4\% + 1\%
&=5\%\end{align}$
The cyclical unemployment in this economy is 2%:

$\begin{align}\text{Cyclical unemployment} &= 7\% - 5\%
&=2\%\end{align}$
An economy is in a recession if the current rate of unemployment is greater than the natural rate of unemployment. Since 7% is greater than 5%, Hamiltonia’s economy is producing less than full employment output.

choices.content: Cyclical unemployment is 2%; output is less than full employment output.
Cyclical unemployment is 2%; output is more than full employment output.
Cyclical unemployment is 0%; output is equal to full employment output.
Cyclical unemployment is 0%; output is less than full employment output.
Cyclical unemployment is 7%; output is equal to full employment output.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What type of unemployment is the deviation of actual unemployment from the natural rate of unemployment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Cyclical unemployment
Structural unemployment
Frictional unemployment
Seasonal unemployment
Distractional unemployment

assessment_items.question_content:
The nation of Jacksonia specializes in producing high-quality cream cheese and fire trucks for export to other countries.

**Which of the following best describes something that would decrease the natural rate of unemployment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Labor unions in both industries become more powerful
Job training programs are eliminated
Improvements in fire truck production mean that more fire truck workers are needed
The economy of Jacksonia expands rapidly due to an increase in the demand for cream cheese domestically and for fire trucks internationally
Cream cheese factories begin to demand skills that are in short supply in Jacksonia

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following would be most likely to cause a decrease in the natural rate of unemployment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Public policies such as unemployment insurance are introduced
An economic boom leads a temporary increase in output
A recession causes widespread layoffs
More people opt for an early retirement
People enter the job market at an earlier age

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements about the natural rate of unemployment is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It can change over time.
It is the sum of cyclical and frictional unemployment.
It is the same as the nonaccelerating inflation rate of unemployment (NAIRU).
It is the sum of cyclical and structural unemployment.
All countries have the same natural rate of unemployment.
